mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase discount rates applied to equity cash flows, directly suppressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rising rate periods are not significantly lower than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.0 percent, labor market slack triggers recession fears that override growth optimism, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in more than 60 percent of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or out-of-sample accuracy drops below 52 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in real personal income and rising consumer prices compress household purchasing power, forcing corporate earnings downgrades and negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint signal fails to predict negative returns with statistical significance (p > 0.05) over a 3-year rolling window, or interaction term coefficient flips positive.

mechanism_narrative: During post-shock recovery phases, positive month-over-month changes in industrial production signal rapid capacity utilization that drives immediate positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no positive correlation with industrial production growth during recovery periods, or Sharpe ratio of the signal remains below 0.5.

mechanism_narrative: Increasing housing starts reflect strong construction sector demand and consumer confidence, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Housing starts growth fails to predict positive 20-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.04, or signal accuracy drops below 50 percent in backtests.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 300.0, entrenched inflation expectations force aggressive central bank tightening, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in over 65 percent of instances when CPI exceeds 300.0, or the threshold effect vanishes in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market expansion, supporting corporate revenue growth and positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal yields a negative or zero Rank IC over rolling 3-year windows, or interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: In early-cycle expansion phases, quarter-over-quarter increases in real GDP signal accelerating economic momentum that reliably produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: GDP growth fails to predict positive 20-day returns during early-cycle periods, or the signal out-of-sample accuracy falls below 51 percent.
